rationale: The two papers have strong construct overlap: the citing paper centers on vice–virtue temptation and self-control utility, while the cited paper develops and tests consumer choice between hedonic and utilitarian goods under different decision frames. Given this direct thematic alignment and the cited paper’s likely role as a foundational theory/empirical source for vice–virtue choice, this pair should not be screened out as a mere incidental mention. Even without in-text citation context, the evidence supports sending it to second-stage review.
